summary: DHH highlights OmniOutliner 2.2's new XML export capabilities that enable integration with Apple's Keynote presentation software. The update allows users to write presentations in OmniOutliner and import them into Keynote, with round-trip editing support. Beyond Keynote, the improved XML engine enables XSLT-based exports to Word-compatible HTML and custom HTML formats. DHH is particularly excited about the potential for a TeX export stylesheet, which would wrap the powerful but unfriendly typesetting system in OmniOutliner's user-friendly interface.
key_points: OmniOutliner 2.2 beta adds XML export enabling seamless integration with Apple Keynote
The XML/XSLT architecture makes OmniOutliner a hub for multiple output formats including Word and custom HTML
Round-trip editing between OmniOutliner, Keynote, and OmniGraffle creates a powerful workflow
A potential TeX export stylesheet could make academic typesetting more accessible
money_quotes: Write your presentations in OO and make Keynote soup it up.
TeX is that unfriendly package of typographic godness being used to produce most of mathematical and computer science reports of the world.
Wrapping TeX in OO would be very, very cool.
key_insight: Proper XML and XSLT support transforms a simple outliner into a powerful interoperability hub, connecting presentation, document, and typesetting tools through standardized data exchange.
